Clerks Called on Carpet in

SPOKANE CLERKS


ARE THERE
Big Sensation Over Annual
Theft of Railroads


Money.

Clerks Called on Carpet in


Minneapolis.

pages.raw_text:
(By United Press Leased Wtre)


MINNEAPOLIS, Jan. 3.-Chief
clerks of several divisions of the
Great Northern railway are on the
carpet here today in an investiga-
tiOn of the recently discovered
frauds, by means of which the, rail-
road is said to have been defrauded
out Of 100000 annually for several
years.


Clerks from Great Falls and
Havre, Mont, and Everett, Seattle
and Spokane were called to go over
the records of their offices with
their superiors. Speaking of the
officials who are being investi-
gated, President Louis Hill said:


THE investigation does not mean
that all the officials called to Min
neapolis are implicated or even sus-
pected. They may not be to blame
for these things, or even connected
with them in any way. They have
been called here to go over the de-
tails of their offices with u.s."


The rip pass frauds are said to
have been made possible through
the connivance of railroad officials
with employment agents, who
transported large numbers of la
bOrers.
